methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Ascend Elements
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Growth), post-money $3B+

data.narrative:
CORE POWER is a Growth-stage climate and energy company founded by Mikal Bøe that has raised $100M+ to date. The valuation universe consists of 9 private peers (including Ascend Elements, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems) and 1 public comparable (LNZA), though a narrower set of same-stage and similar-tier peers is limited.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised of $100M as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $120M. A peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient datapoints within tight filters (fewer than 3 same-stage or adjacent-stage peers at comparable tiers exist), and relying on the full 21-company Climate & Energy sector median would lack specificity to CORE POWER's positioning and stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $102M to $138M, with a median estimate of $120M. This range reflects modest upside from the capital-raised baseline, consistent with typical Growth-stage progression. Key uncertainties include the absence of granular financial metrics (revenue, EBITDA, burn rate) and limited same-stage comparables, which constrains conviction around placement within the derived range. Investors should weight this valuation against proprietary operating data and market positioning relative to the identified peer set.
